mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals an active Fed easing cycle that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS fell from 5.25% in mid-2007 to 2.28% by April 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Once UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points within a 3-month window, it crosses a threshold historically associated with recession onset, triggering rapid equity repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose from 4.4% in early 2007 to 5.0% by April 2008
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE breaches are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 across 2005-2008 sample.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) continue to decline sharply even as FEDFUNDS is cut aggressively, it signals a breakdown in monetary policy transmission through the credit and housing channel, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as the real economy weakens despite easier policy.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell to 947k units in March 2008 from over 1,500k units in 2006
failure_condition: In periods meeting the joint trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus HOUST alone.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in payroll employment growth (PAYEMS trending negative over 3 months) and rising UNRATE confirms an entrenched labor recession, amplifying negative forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change < 0 AND UNRATE 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS declined in Jan-Apr 2008 while UNRATE rose to 5.0%
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails to outperform a simple PAYEMS-only or UNRATE-only model in out-of-sample Rank IC, or 60d forward returns following joint trigger exceed unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects contracting manufacturing output, a leading indicator that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets price in weaker corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value of 111.18 in April 2008 reflected slowing momentum relative to prior quarters
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month momentum and 5-day forward returns stays below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or negative INDPRO momentum periods show non-negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline more than 0.5% month-over-month, it signals an abrupt drop in consumer demand that has historically preceded broad equity market weakness over the subsequent month as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value fell from 378,148M to a revised 364,816M reflecting downward consumer demand revisions
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns following RSAFS trigger events are positive in more than 55% of historical cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across the 2005-2010 sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation runs above 4% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously being cut, the resulting stagflationary policy conflict limits the market's ability to benefit from monetary easing, producing muted or negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as real rates and inflation risk premia rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL rose to 213.9 in April 2008 amid ongoing FEDFUNDS cuts to 2.28%
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint high-inflation/easing periods exceed unconditional historical average returns, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC indistinguishable from zero across multiple stagflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a macro regime where UNRATE is rising while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is simultaneously slowing below trend, the combined signal of labor deterioration and output contraction indicates recession is underway, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month with greater reliability than either indicator alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change > 0 AND GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose to 5.0% by April 2008 amid weakening GDP growth signals in early 2008
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms the UNRATE-only threshold model in walk-forward testing.
